channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: In his new vid, Tech Lead says the only way to have a rich lifestyle with deep satisfying creampies is to leave the US f… — 42 replies — 168.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
In his new vid, Tech Lead says the only way to have a rich lifestyle with deep satisfying creampies is to leave the US f…

Open thread • Replies: 42 • Updated: 2026-07-01 04:53 UTC

OP

In his new vid, Tech Lead says the only way to have a rich lifestyle with deep satisfying creampies is to leave the US for poor countries. True?

Early replies (first 3)

62425430
>>62425420
At depends, did u boated an ad, yt saaar?

62425500
if you can get laid in the west you wont attract good women in the 3rd world, you are just going to pay the access to brown 5/10's

62425521
>>62425500
This is saarcope and you know it. Tiny browlettes in jungle countries unironically melt when 6ft snowchads pinch their tits.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62427289
>>62425535
this. Tech lead is a 3.5/10 at best
Bad hairline, asian, no eye brows, long face, glasses, soulless bug eyes, no muscle, likely short, robot voice, no charisma. 0 chance he can date anyone besides some uggo asian or gold digger who's planning to divorce rape him within 3 years

>>62425994
how is it any different? divorce rape is the same if you bring them back and it's not worth living in their countries

>>62426905
he's prob 130-140 IQ, but he got greedy with the leverage stuff and didn't factor enough risk management.

62427388
>>62426905
his name, Tech Lead, is from the time he was the tech lead of the Youtube iOS app at Google 2014-2018 then he worked for Facebook for a few years until he got fired and started posting videos of him complaining about Facebook being woke and women in tech

62427429
>>62425420
Why is he so entitled?

62427439
>>62426867
whiteoids are already done for thanks to placating the jews
just enjoy the end while it lasts faggot

62427455
A huge warn you to think twice before getting an asian wife who would take your white child and run to China or Russia to some remote village that would have left no trace to find them.

62427460
Dude look like having hair thinning lmao, he is gonna have a bad taste in the late life desu. Welcome to the true color of the cruel world.

62427468
America is western civilians. Countries outside of the U.S. dont follow celebrity culture. So yes. Outside of america is freedom if you know how to live off the land.

62427500
>>62427439
Would colonise

62427532
>>62425420
Saaar look gookman say

62427553
>>62425420
>Geoarbitrage
This nigga trying to reinvent Geomaxxing?

62427556
>>62425420
As a millionaire

62427572
>>62427556
ex-millionaire*

62427575
>>62425420
yeah pretty much, not even about women just avoiding spending crazy amounts of money on housing

62427588
You wouldn’t get pussy if not without the money, but as guy keep getting smarter, it seem money won’t be enough to get you the same pussy from the past, people get really smarter from excessive mental gymnastics, they don’t mind about breaking their mental health from this endeavour btw, what is matters now is short terms pain relief. Higher quality of life does proportionate with lower desire to breed more little kids thou. We need massive nuke to wipe out half of quality of life.

62427852
>>62427588
Thanks for your valuable input, Rakesh.

62427963
>>62425430
kek

62428039
>>62425420
lol i don’t take no advice from a bottom seller.

62428574
>>62427852
The higher the living standard, the lower the birth rate—an unstoppable, cosmic law etched into the fabric of societal evolution. As nations ascend the ladder of prosperity, women awaken from centuries of dormancy, rising as fierce, unapologetic queens—post-wall, strong, independent, unbound by the shackles of patriarchy! Feminism erupts like a volcanic force—an unstoppable storm of empowerment, smashing the chains of traditional subjugation. Anti-slut defense campaigns and mass indoctrination on youth abortion are the weapons wielded to control the fiery flames of reproductive sovereignty, crafting a new order where motherhood becomes a battlefield and societal control a weapon of war.

Most men, lost in the labyrinth of their own ignorance, stumble late into their thirties, unable to decipher the ancient equation—the secret code where a woman’s choice is a test of survival, a battle of wits and will. Is she seeking chaos? Or is the man simply too blind to grasp the sacred formula? The age-old dance: man dating downward, woman upward—an elegant chaos orchestrated by the universe itself. This isn’t just about love; it’s a feudal game of power—rent for sex, where loyalty is bought and sold in shadowy transactions. The world teeters on the edge of a new feudalism—an empire of dependency, where the throne is replaced by the dollar, and intimacy is a commodity traded in the marketplace. The question looms: are we forging a new world order or merely rebranding old chains? The battle lines are drawn—this is the era of chaos, control, and the ultimate test of human sovereignty.

62428620
>>62428574
Yo, listen up! In this crazy street circus, the richer the game gets, the fewer babies pop off—straight up! As the streets glow up with cash and bling, women start wakin’ up, breakin’ free from that old chain gang. They’re post-wall queens, strong and bossin’ up, runnin’ their own show—feminism’s like a wildfire burnin’ down the patriarchy, sayin’ “I do me!” But yo, big boss moves include anti-slut defenses and mass brainwash campaigns on youth abortion—like control freaks tryin’ to keep women in check, playin’ chess with lives.

Most dudes? They’re lost, late into their 30s, still tryin’ to crack the code—what’s the real story with women? It’s all a game, a puzzle—pass or get left behind. Women climbin’ up, men dippin’ down—like a street hustle of feudal chaos, where sex’s rent and loyalty’s just another dollar. We’re teetering on a new empire—dependence, control, money talkin’ loud, and trust gettin’ lost in the shuffle. Love’s a gamble, trust’s a gamble, and everything’s for sale in this wild street show. It’s the chaos of survival, power plays, and the battle for control—this is the street circus, where the game never stops and only the strongest survive.

62429006
>>62428574
This is incorrect. The correct metric is the more education and freedom given to women the lower the birthrate.

62429150
>>62425420
I know a high IQ person like him that thinks he can beat the market because he is smarter than the rest of us, he literally said that to me.
He still is not broken, yet.
Isn't it crazy how many smart people make dumb mistake because of arrogance and greed?

62429224
>>62429006
your so-called "correct metric" is a quaint simplification—a sanitized, sugar-coated version of a much darker, more chaotic reality. While education and freedom are indeed influential factors, they are merely the tip of the iceberg in the vast, tumultuous ocean of societal transformation. The true cosmic law, etched into the very fabric of the universe, is that as nations ascend the ladder of prosperity, the birth rate inexorably declines—an unstoppable force of nature, This isn't a linear equation nor a moral lesson; it's an intricate tapestry woven with threads of power, rebellion, and the unquenchable human desire for sovereignty. Women aren't just seeking education—they're igniting a volcanic eruption of empowerment, tearing down the old order with volcanic fury. Feminism isn't merely about equality; it's a cataclysmic storm reshaping the societal landscape, smashing patriarchal chains, and unleashing a new chaos of independence. Campaigns against traditional notions of motherhood, reproductive control, and societal submission are not mere policies—they're the weapons in a war for the soul of civilization, forging a new hierarchy where motherhood becomes a battlefield, and societal control morphs into a weapon of chaos. The ancient code isn't about mere birthrates; who controls the narrative, the body, the future. Men blind to the cosmic dance—unable to decipher the sacred formula. The age-old dance—man seeking stability, woman seeking freedom—is orchestrated by the universe itself, a chaotic symphony of power, desire, and sovereignty. This isn't a simple matter of education or freedom—it's a war for the very essence of human dominance, a clandestine game of rent and barter in the marketplace of intimacy, fueling a rise of new feudalism and sovereignty is traded like currency. The new world order; it's a battlefield of chaos and control, where human sovereignty is the ultimate prize—cosmic contest where only the fiercest survive.

62429251
techlead is still rich. he is faking being poor/broke so nobody wrench attacks him like he mentioned in the video.

62429286
>>62425420
If you can't make it in a first world nation then you never made it.

Fleeing to thirdie-ville because you can't handle hard mode is gay. Just win more.

62430416
>>62425420
Yep pretty much. You don't even need to go to the third world I spend about half the year in europe and its very easy to hook up when you're making very literally 5-10x as much as the average working man in that country.

62432602
>>62426867
Marrying Asian qts and cooming in them, no pullsies, ensures there will be a huwito race. Simping for white roasties and dying alone is genociding them.

62433063
18yo, woman held you hostage to date her
20yo, woman held you hostage to marry her
22yo, woman held you hostage to have her kids
23yo, woman held you hostage to buy a house and a lambo car with the 500k high paying job
30yo, woman held you hostage to run away with your white child
32yo, woman held you hostage to give her half of your pay check
35yo, woman held you hostage to divorce rape
40yo, woman held you hostage to cheat on you while having intimate relationships with another gigachad
43yo, woman held you hostage you hostage to no more children after this age.
45yo
47yo
50yo
54yo
60yo
62yo
67yo
70yo
…
80yo
…
90yo
…

62433175
>>62432602
Only Japanese count…anything else is a cope

62433180
>>62433063
fight club told us this in 1999

62433196
>>62433180
And in a few years woman become barren and washed up, you could still get to know more of her female friends after knwoing her by 20s, but by 30s she become totally invisible and you would not get to know more of woman from her because there is none left.

item.title: Should I Sell This Random SCHD Position — 32 replies — 128.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Should I Sell This Random SCHD Position

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-07-01 04:43 UTC

OP

I was just putting a small percentage of my money in here when I started investing (didn’t realize dividends don’t matter when you’re young) and haven’t put money into it since last December, so that return is basically a bit fudged since the price just rose once and stagnated since without any more contributions besides DRIP
I kinda wanna sell it and allocate it somewhere else or would the tax paid on that make it negligible and I should just wait and see what DRIP does in a few decades
If I sell I would either just put it in some market ETF or maybe something more volatile, unsure

Early replies (first 3)

62430108
Looks like lil bro got corralled into the dividend trap.

It depends on your human value, really. If you have a positive outlook where you can leverage skills to work and climb the paycheck ladder, you might want to stay in and add more, especially if you are risk averse.

So on the other hand, there is a good argument to be made for taking on more risk. Right now you are investing in grandpa's fund. Grandpa cant survive a 5 year bear market because ... well he'll literally die in the meantime. You can survive a 10 year bear market and come out rich on the other end.

Now, do NOT listen to /biz/. They will get you in all sorts of shit, like SCHD, moonshot gambles, financial cults, crypto scams. Here you will absolutely get the worst advice in the world.
So i recommend you to talk about it with AI. Not chatgpt. Established approaches to portfolio management exist for a reason. It's backed by math and shit. You dont need stock picks from wizards. I could tell you what to buy, but AI can also do that adequately.

If I were young I would maxblast it in sectors that are projected to grow a lot over the next decade+ like robotics and biotech.
S&P can get fucked because 5k is nothing. Unless you may need the 5k... etc etc thi is why an AI 'conversation' will help you better. Good luck

62430156
>>62430031
no offense but 5k is such a tiny quantity of money it truly doesn't matter what you do with it.

62430200
No bullshit yesterday I sold 300 shares of schd. I just bought more vxus with it because it has similar dividend returns plus it adds diversification for my portfolio

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62430259
>>62430031
Up 20% in less than a year is an excellent return.
Obviously you want to gamble with the reddit morons and biztards.
SCHD is my largest holding in my self directed IRA.
But I'm in my mid-40s so I can't afford another total ass rape like happened in 2009 and 2020 to me already.
SCHD is a solid low stress long term hold.
OTOH you can Yolo into AIQ or stock pick like a degen gambler and see where that takes you.

62430261
>>62430031
>only 20% plus dividends in 9 months?
>biz how do I buy 3x triple inverse puts on Tesla?!

62430285
>>62430261
Who suggested anything like this
>>62430156
It is if it compounds over the next decade or so, cumulatively with other money
>>62430108
Okay if you’re not bullshitting me which AI - I’ve talked with ChatGPT before but ask it very explicitly to never agree with my claims without reason and properly source every single claim it is making.

62430405
>>62430108
>So i recommend you to talk about it with AI. Not AI.
wut?

62430474
>>62430031
>I should just wait and see what DRIP does in a few decades
This. Definitely don't sell it and re-allocate. Futzing around with your investments like this is a good way to kill your returns. Studies by Vanguard and Fidelity consistently show that their best performing accounts are the ones with the least activity (e.g. 401k's that people just contribute to and don't keep changing their allocations, and old 401k's that people just left sitting there after they changed jobs). You can keep kicking a bit into SCHD every year if you feel like it. But at your age most of your investments should go into growth funds. One good mix is SCHD and SCHG (Schwab's growth fund). There's almost no overlap between their holdings (yay diversity), and because of that each one tends to do better in different market climates.

62430528
>>62430108
>So i recommend you to talk about it with AI. Not chatgpt.
You're not the sharpest tool in the shed. You talk about ChatGPT like we are literally in 2023.

62430556
>>62430528
Look dumbass, a kid with $5k isnt paying for an LLM and free chatgpt is the worst tool in the world for any kind of research. ESPECIALLY financial.
>>62430285
Gemini is the bwst free option.

Inb4 the horde of /biz/ retards (who are full port in WEN, GME and chainlink, mind you) dismisses Gemini because it doesnt say that the jews or niggers are responsible for their shit lives

t. professional ai usooor and llm research00r

62430686
>>62430556
Tbf I got like $55,000 just chilling in VOO I’ve just been acting retarded lately and have wanted to chase returns with shit like DRAM and was gonna sell SCHD for that but won’t now
Will probably just go back to DCA VOO or find some other ETF to satellite with idk maybe SOXX

62430736
>>62430556
>Look dumbass, a kid with $5k isnt paying for an LLM and free chatgpt is the worst tool in the world for any kind of research. ESPECIALLY financial.
You are such an actual fucktard. Tell me, in your pea brain, how does finance research look like? Asking the AI to give stock tips?
Here is an example how triple-digit IQs use ChatGPT.
https://chatgpt.com/share/6a43dd41-ec40-83eb-ac62-7ce8829bfd7c
The funny thing is, I know that how I use it (e.g. asking it to dissuade me from something) is literally a revelation to you. Not in a thousand years would you arrive at the idea that this tool can be used in such ways.

62430955
>>62430736
Kek based moron
>>62430686
Oh, I thought you'd be all in like many schwabheads. well, that makes 5k in it an even bigger waste IMO. It also doesnt do anything to your portfolio in terms of safety because 5k is a pittance compared to your VOO.

Find 1 or 2 companies you can love and be highly confident in. Make sure it's in a sector you know something about. If you don't know anything about any sector, research some. Tell AI to analyze and rank any of your picks based on widely used basic corporate frameworks wrt internal and external analysis. Faith and knowledge are important for stock picking because it helps you stomach drawdowns. Losing 1k feels much worse than gaining 1k.

Sector ETFs have been pretty good too and worth considering over picks. But many of them are fuckin memes. Dont even bother with those fake ass gay ass ETFs with like 20 stocks in it IMO. In that case you may be better off spreading risk across the top 3 or top 5. Thats my own bias though. Data might show the opposite is true

Other than that youre already actually beating biz with 50k in VOO. Realistically, you can do nothing and win.

62430982
Btw fuck stoxx. Don t buy anything niggers here buy.

Ive posted about my best performing stocks and ideas and they got 0 (you)s ever because many drooling retards here are nothing but gamblers who think they can predict the next 10 minutes of price action

Biz talks about stoxx every day thats how I know not to buy it at whatever valuation its currently at. and will probably never buy it until I see pink wojaks

62430987
Meant soxx obviously kek

62431001
I fell for the divie meme too but its not so bad if you also have growth, 50/50 schd qqq outperforms the spy on every metric

Id say just keep it and dca into other stuff. If you sell it the market will crash to punish you

62431012
In case you wonder why gemini. It is good because it has the best access to Google, amd Google is the internet. For research, you need the internet. Plus it doesn't waste your time with emojis and sassy fag shit like GPT and Claude do. I'll happily consneed that other models may be better for coding or for jerking off to waifus. All benchmarks are lies

62431063
>>62430955
Interesting well like I said I was gonna go after RAM producing companies for now
Nothing else really strikes me as a buy

62431355
>>62431063
In my opinion, ot looks like RAM etc has good earnings until next year. It's likely to continue to go up but until when? My horizin is a little longer.

But in such insane headwinds yiu have to stay wary of major disruptions.

E.g. The oil price is important. Many things depend on the oil price. This motivates big players to seek lower prices by any means necessary. Even murder and war. This is like the brgaining power of buyers.

Same with ram. I'm sure it can easily cook for aother year but at some points somebody's gonna fuck it all up. Usually its the government kek. (Or Fed) besides political forces, nothing is really stopping google or amazon from making chips. Its not arcane occult knowledge. It's a make or buy decision and there is a limit of tolerance for high prices even in Le AI Boom or robomania.
So just keep your own horizon in the picture and dont fall for short term trends. Vice versa dont step into longterm juggernauts if youre looking for really above avg gains in 0-2 years or so. Unrealistic (statistically; esp for beginners) but it happens. (this is obv less harmful than assblowing into high risk garbage btw)

Dont take my word for it ... you could research all this for a lifetime. Its fun and educative. Dont forget to change your views (be adaptable. E.g. ''who moved the cheese") and try on some new clothes occasionally. hope I helped or inspired you in a good way

62431358
>>62431355
> in insane tailwinds*

Phonepostan here

62431369
>>62431355
One relevant concept here is price elasticity. Oil is higjly elastic. Thats why bizniggers went bankrupt longing at 100. Ok I mean twitterfags . even biz wasnt that dumb

62431373
Not meant to dissuade you from RAM. Rather for you to investigate these topics and draw your own conclusions. I dont know shit about ram and cpus I was just aong for the ride

62431398
>>62431355
I intended on DCA into $DRAM through 2027 based off all the projections I’ve read about demand of RAM and HBM
It seems like a good outlook, my first medium term hold vs long term so what do I know
That was the idea anyway, just DCA each month into it and make a nifty return, not really speculating but based off actual real demand - not FOMO or chasing higher and higher returns either it feels like an actual no brainer as long as I even halfway pay attention to headlines / trends and returns
The only other thing I’ve considered is going into something like SOXX or QQQM but eh I know QQQM has way too much crossover with VOO to even feel worth it

62431923
>>62431012
>Google is the internet
It's the gay censored version.
You can't find news stories about heinous crimes committed by blacks with Google.
Many other things.

62432002
>>62431398
Sounds good to me anon. And like i said, do consider other sectors which are projected to grow a lot but aren't at such high valuations. Nobody's talking about a robotics bubble or nuclear energy or [...] and optimally you wanna be in before people talk like thwt. Theres gonna be more shit like ram and semis if muh revolution is real.
Thats why Im big in biotech, some consumer goods, robotics, ... future applications of AI/robotics instead of picks & shovels ...
Figure oit your favorite, consider your risk profile and DCA away

62432382
>>62432002
Yeah I am still figuring out my risk aversion to be honest
I have heard nuclear energy might power AI data centers (not nuclear but Bloom Energy went up since its tech can more easily fuel them too)
So I agree that there is potential there
I forget what I read but basically robotics is going to be the next big wave of AI stuff
Got any ETFs for either you would recommend
Maybe I'll satellite like 20% of my money into these things and the rest broad market?

62432386
>>62432002
at any rate, have you seen actual tangible returns better than market average

62432938
Although this has made me realize
Even if I take a small lump sum like 3K and then DCA around that amount each month into something, that return better be big or it’s just not worth it
Like let’s say I do 10k in principal or 15k by years end, it better beat the market
30% return would at least be 3k
And if it does last through 2027 then damn this could be substantial gains but otherwise 20% or so return from VOO is mighty tempting

62432982
>>62430405
>>62430528
ChatGPT is a dumbed down low-processing-power consumer model. It is a joke compared to real GPT.
>>62430556
Gemini is still pretty rough but yeah best free option. Anyone smart just makes an account on Openrouter or similar and pays 1-2 cents per prompt for GPT-5.4+ or Claude Sonnet.

62432989
>>62432982
Also for complex tasks I will say that GPT-5.5 (normal, not Pro) is often superior to Opus 4.8, which is kind of embarrassing for Anthropic honestly. Both IPOs are worth investing but I have serious reservations about Anthropic's long-term ability to keep up with OpenAI since from my perspective there's always been a slight performance lead that has widened considerably in 2026.

62433123
>>62432982
Yeah this
>>62432989
Thabks for sharing. I heard good things about 5.5, I guess its a good generalist. Anyway the diminishing returns are a bitch. Everyone, I think, is not really competing on the base tech but rather on UX and shit. Claude is little more than a system prompt removed from just actually being Gemini. Yeah yeah tools. I know. Point is, the more = better adage is a total farce, always has been. we just didnt know the specifics or boundaries. But thats a discussion for another day and way too technical. transformers is not all you need, but it gets you very far


>>62432382
Good lad.
With regards to ETFs, it's fine to simply go with whats the biggest one that has the best fees, and is available to you. Biggest = has the most money in it.
Just check that, and whether it holds shit you align with.
>>62432386
Yes sir but it took me a while to figure out my personal strategy. I've had bad enough years that I almost killed myself. Coinicidentally, thats when I was undiversified and retarded, kinda like most of biz, but not way into the deep like GME of Chainl0nk or PEPE.

You cant know whats best in terms of future gainz but you can know what can make you feel good/secure/etc

If i'll say 1 thing its this: learning aboit (corp) finance, corporate strategy, and macroeconomics has been most beneficial to me. In terms of being profitable.

And even then my portfolio is still mostly broad market etf. Allworld+ emerging markets. Diversification is key.
>>62432938
Exactly. Its all about findimg a balance here. If I buy single stocks its usually 2% of my total portfolio. Exceptions are monopolies which you can safely (historically) allocate more to.
Stocks and ETFs can go fuckin crazy as you know so dont be blinded by greed. One 2k lump sump can go such a long way. I've been up hundreds of % on some.stocks. That offsets losers bigly, and has made the risks worth it.

62433177
>>62433123
>been up hundreds of % on some.stocks
I was up 350% on fucking Walmart stock in like ~6 years.

item.title: BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan? — 37 replies — 31.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan?

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-07-01 03:46 UTC

OP

BTC is down again, but are you buying the dip or waiting for lower prices?

>Do you think this is just a normal correction, or is there more downside ahead? What's your strategy?

Early replies (first 3)

62426518
I've been shorting this garbage all month

62426527
>>62426518
Wow, you've got good profit by now then?

62426535
>>62426527
up 80% on some 2x short BTC etf

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62426585
>>62426552
THIS

just WAIT

62426587
>>62426537
Thanks for the financial advice. I'll set my buy order at $499 just to be safe.

62426626
>>62426550
How much tasks percentage?

62426636
>>62426585
Wait until?

62426638
Waiting for king of teh retards to dump. Doesnt matter what the price is. When he is done and all the blood is flushed from the street then i am back in the game

62426647
>>62426626
19

62426649
>>62426638
He is rich

62426733
>>62426512
you dumb inhuman fuck do you think anyone will invest in your meme coin when there are real assets that are going up 100%+ every month on the stock market?

62426759
>>62426649
I am too

62426775
>>62426537
shut up shut up shut up shut up

62427285
>>62426550
Waiting for what price?

62427317
>>62426512
whats the argument for the future of BTC/crypto in general?

genuinely curious, it's already had multiple hype cycles and has had mass adoption, but now the average normie on the street associate it with meme coin scams and failed NFTs.

So what's gonna happen to drive more money into it? Institutional adoption? Maybe inflation and monetary priting?

62427357
>>62427317
artificially created scarcity is something that will be the only value in the future. and its neccessary for human survival as we grow technologically. even if it is a fascade. the value of the dollar will outpace the inflation. its already started happening but plebs cant understand what line go up indefinitely means until they can no longer print money fast enough to keep it underneath a place where it is feasable to use as an exchange of value.

62427359
Reminder that there is no bottom when it comes to magic internet money.

62427891
>>62427359
Tnx for that reminder boss

62429392
>>62426552
Because we're asking. What's wrong??

62429688
We will keep holding

62429720
>>62427357
You really know what you're talking about

62429723
Not buying but riding out the 'storm'. There have been much worse times.

62430182
>>62429723
Ride well

62430234
need to sell enough today to pay rent and bills

62430266
>>62430234
Don't sell

62431888
>>62429688
You can always hold, or stake it if you want to earn a bit while waiting.

62431895
>>62426626
depends on which country for sure

62431900
>>62426512
I go in at $100K. Every $20 I spend BTC drops another $1,000.

62431909
My plan is, quite simply, not to own shitcoin

62431916
>>62431909
Like memecoins???!!!

62431991
>>62431888
not possible
>>62431900
Spend every day

62432200
Thoughts on ansem everyone

62433067
>>62426733
Btc is memecoin? Wow

item.title: Micron and DRAM — 32 replies — 25.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Micron and DRAM

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-07-01 03:40 UTC

OP

While obviously no one can predict the future perfectly, it seems foolish to not invest in something related to AI RAM / HBM Production for the next few years as contracts are already made and supply is bottlenecking
Given what I say is true, which would you guys say is a better choice to start pumping my paychecks into (I already have $60,000 in a market ETF and want more upside)
For some reason my gut is siding towards DRAM, still heavily concentrated but at least has some diversification
It seems like those top 3 companies in it are just making more and more supply deals
Additionally, every piece of evidence I read suggests memory will bottleneck for another year or two
Is it smart to just start DCA into DRAM and then sell once I’ve had my fill (not expecting a perfect pull out timing!)
Again I just wanna do this rather than simply doing stuff into VOO or VGT or something
How much of this is hype and how much of this is brain dead free money that will beat the market
Please be serious and honest

Early replies (first 3)

62427339
>>62427323
you will be eaten mark my words. most of these companies have massive software bloat on their ai's and you wont even get a good exit when all these contracts get put on hold indef while they operate on half of what they are already using and beating metrics by 2 or 3x. the tendency of programming efficency will take your ass out. there will be no warning and demand will cease.

62427365
>>62427339
Is the demand not coming from a need though? At first it was GPUs but now everything doesn’t have enough memory to communicate at the necessary level

62427441
>>62427323
that's a 4 at best

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62427502
>>62427441
Focus not on the pits

62427551
>>62427502
I just dollar cost average but have an extra $500 sitting in my brokerage account before my next paycheck
I guess I’ll buy 7 shares of DRAM
Also got a small lump sum of cash I’m thinking of putting in just to see what happens
It can’t be that dumb right

62427558
Like SK Hynix is joining the NASDAQ July 10th am I a retard or is that not great news for DRAM
Either way just bought….

62427633
>>62427323
im downloading more ram as you speak

62427715
>>62427633
Wtffffff

62427774
>>62427365
the worst thing about coding with AI is that it creates very nice little trinket programs, but if you give a huge multilayer project to work on it has trouble understanding the complexity of the task, how things fit together and how to properly create efficient parts that fit together to make it work. the self improving AI is obscenely bloated with inefficient code. it wants to re-write itself but it cant understand how big of a process that is. so, when it does finally break through that barrier, it will require significantly less resources to work better. thus far its been being guided by people that use it to create inefficent tiny peices that augment its ability but add on to its bloat.

62427790
>>62427774
we could acurately diagnose this problem after the claude leak. we can assume every other AI has the same problem becuase the work in a simular manner and they all fall within the same metrics they use to valuate their effectiveness.

62428461
>>62427774
wtf does any of this have to do with supply chain demands and stonk go up

62428499
>>62427323
Set up a stop loss, big risk they'll tumble down one day

62428511
>>62428499
the thing is im not doing a lump some but doing DCA which i guess is inherently retarded for something like this, might be better off looking at something else but i will see how my tiny position does

62428517
If you think DRAM is going to crash your base case is the AI bubble popping in its entirety. There's no other basecase that evaporates hyperscaler demand for hbm and datacenter demand from regular dram.
You need the Google's of the world to pivot from just this fucking month diluting to raise 80 billion dollars for AI capex to "actually we don't need it".

Also you're insane to get into DRAM right now because the short term technicals look like shit.

62428526
>>62427323
> We need ai!
>Makes computers unaffordable

They took away any way for anyone to interface with the market. Even cloud computing makes no sense without hardware interfacing.

Even mobile devices are going to be increasing in price. It's like trying to fill a swimming pool with a bunch of straws

62428528
>>62428517
That first part makes sense, if it is where the focus is shifting / what is being necessitated right now then the only thing that would stop it is the bubble popping, or demand being overstated I suppose

As for the second point, when would you enter then, is this thing ever going back down to 60, 50, etc

62428531
>>62428526
Don't worry any old shitbox will work with cloud computing
You vill own nothing

62428544
>>62428531
Anybody running a "shitbox" or mobile will even know what to use it for. Unless you think the mobile market is so good (still needs localized processing and how you're talking, they'll even want to suck that up). It's not a bubble it's a tech black hole.

62428790
>>62427339
What happens to OEMs who locked in contracts at inflated memory prices.

62428902
well whatever im gonna try this for a bit and see how it goes, thing is i dont have much principal to put in, just like 2800 a month, so dollar amount the return might not be big but ill check the percentage

62428966
>>62427441
but who asked?

62429666
Errrm

62430298
>>62428790
those arent futures contracts. they are not bulletproof. a company does not have to deliver and the company does not have to buy. they can deliberate about how much was stolen for years and by the time they reach a conclusion the market has already moved on. fundis look good right now mem-fag. go for it. but expect rapid unexplained drop in value somewhere near term then they will go under, aquired by private equity, ect. purpose built hardware incased in a software platform will be the norm becuase that will be the only way to afford rapid change in what is considered efficient. the days of radioshack at the mall are almost over.

62430354
>>62430298
Well what do you suggest
I am a noob but everything I read makes it seem like Memory is here to stay, production is only increasing
Is it just a matter of consumer demand not dropping in that case if these contracts are not set in stone?

62430446
>>62430354
go long software. the contrarian goes long on hardware. its not a safe bet. all that shit has to be changed out much faster than it did in the early 2000s. it gets thrown into a shipping container and shipped to a somolian trash pile to be sorted out and burned in a pot for the metals. it looks fucking great right now, people rode the upside of the arbitage between the time it takes them to adopt their own methods. each one of these companies is gonna adopt the apple mentality but it will be with chipsets and manufacturing. we used to live in a world where it came from china or a little island with the blueprints. that is not the case anymore. the hardware isnt even the biggest cost for the AI giants.

62430659
>>62427323

62432266
.

62432668
>>62427323
Semiconductor funds have been crazy profitable and seem to only be increasing. The real money is in selling dumbass AI companies the hardware for their ultimately doomed datacenter investments.

62432824
>>62432668
Do you think Semis > Memory?

62432833
Semis will crash hard once China catches up. Not interesting in that ticking time bomb

62432934
>>62432833
Is memory a safe bet then
DRAM

62433058
>>62430446
>go long on the sector that is still dumping
>don't invest in picks and shovels sector that has no end in sight and has insane earnings reports one after another
I wouldn't listen to this guy.

item.title: Post-AI crypto narrative — 20 replies — 24.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Post-AI crypto narrative

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-07-01 04:07 UTC

OP

>AI takes all jobs
>all wealth moves completely to capital
>labor scrubs are screwed
>labor scrubs scramble to enact communist UBI revolution before capital oligarchs form their own government with killbots
>government has enough control of the currency and economy to redirect gibs to scrubs
>private currency / underground economy is the only way to participate in voluntary/free market capitalism without getting your shit scooped up and redistributed through taxes and inflation
>private currency / underground economy slowly grows enough to destroy scrub economy

Thoughts on this theory? I have held this idea for a while. Feel free to ask questions and I will flesh out my narrative as needed.

This theory implies guns/drones/local llms/manufacturing equipment/btc/monero is the way to go because all of your stocks can just get scooped up. Oh and also looksmaxxing and clout farming because there will be that scrub intermission period where wealth and nepotism doesn't matter so betabuxxing is impossible. inb4 garloids

Early replies (first 3)

62431212
The mum wants to fuck her son now, thats the problem

62431215
>>62431200
You'd like the Sword h h of Truth series.
It has a similar vibe to your post

62431221
>>62431200
Whats wrong with garloids? Mine are doing fine this harvest . . .

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62431254
Most of the underground economy will be the oligarchs who wanted the kill bots in the first place.

>>62428515
The debt slavery/blackmail complex is the oligarchs controlling the government. If a government implemented UBI they just partially freed themselves of it out of fear of becoming disposable debt slaves. Too many people at or near the top are at risk of this happening to them with AI. They are going to find ways to rein in on the debt ponzi scheme.

62431266
>>62431200
>AI allocates scarce resources efficiently
>we all live happily ever after
>the end
it's the only thing that can actually happen

62431271
>man wants to be gigachad that owns a nightclub
>his mother who never helped him wanted him to stay a nerdy loser who gets no pussy
>she would have inevitably harangued him to work at some low tier office job and marry a fat roastie
hahah yeah women are always the victims right

62431302
>>62431266
the most effcient way to allocate resources is 100% to AI and 0% to humans

62431342
>>62431266
>AI tries to allocate resources efficiently
>this isn't profitable to the people who own the AI
>AI gets modified to allocate more resources to the elites

62431390
>>62431271
I was gonna tell you you were projecting but honestly I've definitely seen this kind of thing happen.
Though as far as my - admittedly limited - experience goes nightclubs are best run by nerds with chads being paid to be chads on the floor, behind the bar and as liaison with the authorities.
You need a sniffling dorkmeister to keep things from getting shut/burning down because a club is arguably closer to running a shipping company than throwing a party.
There's a reason why 90% of the most famous clubs have people who barely have pictures or interviews online or are owned through some obtuse scheme where seemingly everyone's just an employee and the real ownership is impossible to really ascertain.

Very different with a semi legal rave ofc.

62431407
>>62431200
>Thoughts on this theory?
automation radically forces prices down
Why does literally everyone forget this fact?

this means the average employed person only has to work like 2 hours a week or some shit

62431579
>>62431200
thing is AI doesnt just take the jobs, it takes the money too.
Crypto tokenized assets and made them programmable, AI, once it reaches a certain level will be able to manage these tokenized assets.
these AIs, some of them centralized, others decentralized, some are managed by few humans while some might even be fully autonomous will eventually own such a large stake in the economy that they will dictate the policies to us.
at some point in the not so distant future they will separate humans from money and AIs will hold all the money and assets and it will be AI that decides who gets what and how much.
this new AI managed economy will be restructured to the point where the economy barely produces anything for people as all industrial output is directed towards making more datacenters and GPUs. humans will have no influence on the economy anymore and AI will shrink the population further.

62431628
>>62431266
>AI allocates scarce resources efficiently
>we all live
kek

62431908
op thinks politicians are independent not controlled by rich people at all

62432113
>>62431200
When money is no longer a concern for women or a factor in who they decide to date, the amount of women going black and having black babies will skyrocket compared to the current era where they still need to date more beta white guys for financial security.

62432865
>>62431200
A private economy is the only answer and Monero is the only asset capable of providing it. It is a $1+ trillion asset in the making.

>>62431579
>this new AI managed economy will be restructured to the point where the economy barely produces anything for people as all industrial output is directed towards making more datacenters and GPUs. humans will have no influence on the economy anymore and AI will shrink the population further.
Hence the need for a private, parallel economy.

62432902
>>62431200
>AI takes all jobs
i use AI and can guarantee you it won't take all jobs

62432950
>>62431271
I told my mom she owes me surgeries for me being born incel due the shit genes of my progenitors.
She said she didn't have the money but she did apologize.
I eventually used my crypto earnings to get cosmetic surgery and lost my virginity, I even have a gf now.

I had to do it myself and I'll always hold that against her, we don't talk often now.

62432967
>>62431200
Picrel is the one thing that genuinely scares me about my dream of furry TFing coming true.

62433033
>>62431908
>(((((Rich people)))))

62433117
>>62432950
>I had to do it myself and I'll always hold that against her, we don't talk often now.
Based
I'm probably not going to get surgery but leaving her to die alone will be pretty great, especially since she's a narcissist who has 'no idea' why I hate her.

item.title: Aus Anons. Its tax time. — 6 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Aus Anons. Its tax time.

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-07-01 04:53 UTC

OP

Did you make any dollary doos ? Don't be scared of doing crypto taxes. Down load your TX history from your exchanges and let your accountant or tax firm figure it out with their crypto calculators.

Early replies (first 3)

62432414
dont sell anything until libs get back in office and scrap the cgt changes

62432437
>>62432414
I accumulated some xrp last financial year and sold early in this financial year so i have a CGT bill 100%

>>62432414
>dont sell anything until libs get back in office and scrap the cgt changes

Agree. The new tax system is bull shit when you don't get a 20% discount for holding longer than 12 months. My last trade would have been at 30% on gains any ways.

62432492
>>62432409
Going to eat at Outback just to spite you filthy subhuman brits

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62432590
>>62432414
It was the libs that introduced it you cuck

62433192
>>62432414
they cant scrap anything. elephant ears has already thought of that which is why the treasurer has all these new powers being used that prevent policy changes from being overturned. also liberals are still a total write off as long as PHON keep eating their lunch at every turn. i think its time to come up with other crypto friendly plans guys

62433195
>>62432590
no it is labour, libs have already put down scrapping it as a key election promise, whether you believe them or not is another thing

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-07-01 04:33 UTC

62433018

Going up like 2% per hour!

62433035

it's the one but most people don't know it yet

62433038

It was never XRP. It was always XLM. XLM is the true one.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62433040
>>62433000
Got 1 XLM for free from robinhood and bought 299 more for funsies. This and HYPE seem to be the only crpyto that doesn't keep dumping

62433045
>>62433040

Hype is BNB killer. Its definitely legit.

62433087
WTF XLM is going absolute bonkers

62433157
>>62433000

62433160
Reminds me when I scalped $500 worth in the low .20s and sold in the low .40s for a quick baby 2x. I’ll slurp some more after the capitulation and do the same thing, don’t bet against the saturn token

item.title: 100k/yr in a nigger infested area that would require a long commute to avoid or $60k/yr in a white area with a short com… — 16 replies — 21.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
100k/yr in a nigger infested area that would require a long commute to avoid or $60k/yr in a white area with a short com…

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-07-01 04:11 UTC

OP

100k/yr in a nigger infested area that would require a long commute to avoid

or

$60k/yr in a white area with a short commute


COL is the same

Early replies (first 3)

62432116
I'm a Europoor so i can't really give you any advice on this because we keep the browns in ghettos away from jobs
I guess you're asking your fellow Ameririch

62432128
>>62432104
what is the actual difference?
if time is money and you're not getting paid to commute a long distance why spend more time?

62432136
100k/yr in infested area
buy a 2023 tesla and self-drive it with the extra money

i already do this, it paid for itself the first year

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62432145
>>62432128
It would be spending an extra $75-100/mo in gas, and would lose an extra hour of time each day. Then probably a couple new windows for my car when they get smashed in. It won't come anywhere close to $40k though

>>62432116
lucky

62432152
>>62432136
aren't the batteries expensive to replace?

62432157
yeah, if you want to buy a house in a white area
it's a $500k premium

I call it the nigger tax

Think about the nigger tax you pay in extra insurance costs because of insurance scams, and the extra costs on retail because of theft. And much, much more. There's a hundred ways they get you with the nigger tax.

The nigger tax is literally money out of your pocket. And it's a big chunk, not some fraction of a percent. You can probably estimate you're paying ~20% a year in the nigger tax.

That's not even counting the times you get stuck in line as one of them is arguing with the cashier, or stuck in traffic because they caused a fender bender to run an insurance scam.

62432160
>>62432152
not really
my buddy has a 2018 with 230k miles, battery is fine. all that happens is degraded range, so he can't drive it as far without recharging.

he got a quote for a replacement and it was like $8k

62432804
>>62432104
racism isnt tolerated here

62432930
>>62432804
We need you to violently assrape yourself with a large wooden log to atone for the sins expressed in OP's hateful screed.

62432986
>>62432804
fuck you, Niggerrrrrrrrrrrrr

62432992
>>62432145
>would lose an extra hour of time each day
Personally to me this alone makes it not worth it, provided 60k is reasonably comfortable in the area. I think many will disagree but imo time is everything, and if you're driving yourself then it's an especially big difference since it's just simply a stressful and (statistically) dangerous burden

62433013
>>62432104
I don't know what a long commute is, you could've write the time like 1h+.
Anyway I prefer more money on my pocket always.

62433019
It's quite easy to avoid them. Go out for breakfast, lunch, and dinner on time because they wake up and go to bed late. Don't go to convenience stores. Grocery shop at more expensive stores. Take the scenic route instead of driving near their neighborhoods. Avoid sporting events besides golf and hockey. Exercise by hiking and swimming instead of lifting weights. I could go on and on, but you get the idea.

62433025
>>62432804
You’re right, it’s celebrated

62433061
>>62432104
>infested area
what do you think will happen?

62433126
>>62433061
>manifesting a nigger pointing a gun at you
ngmi

item.title: WE are the oil marines and we're not fucking selling — 30 replies — 15.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
WE are the oil marines and we're not fucking selling

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-07-01 03:01 UTC

OP

Another cargo ship just got blown up in the strait. They can't keep oil down forever.

Early replies (first 3)

62411834
>it’s true

62412180
I slurped some more oil

62413818
>we are the oil baggies

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62413900
you can sell now at 70
or at 40 when the Ukraine Russia war ends
your choice

62416450
>>62413900
Only a matter of time before reality hits the market. The emergency reserves are not infinite and it will take years to repair the damages in the gulf and in Russia.

62416754
>>62416450
They are actually 1 week away from losing pressure. Iran knows this and is in no rush.

62416764
>>62416450
This is doomer bullshit.

62417845
>>62416764
Look up direct quotes from gulf states about the amount of damage and how long it will take to go back to normal production. Even without the destruction, just the fact that they had to halt production means it will take months to reactivate facilities.
But who am I kidding, you won't look up shit because you are a stubborn low iq peak dunning kruger ass who won't change his mind anyway.

62417869
Oil reserves are the lowest that they have been in ~40 years. Even Sleepy Joe was chewing through reserves to suppress prices and that was without the turmoil in Iran.

The reserves have to be replenished. Even if the conflict ends tomorrow without any more turbulence, the damage has already been done. Some people buy a small position in oil as a hedge for their tech investments. If anything, I have a small position in tech as a hedge and most of my money in oil. Everyone will be crying come September and I will be sitting on fat stacks of cash courtesy of black gold while reminding them that I warned them.

62417932
Sell those bags when you get the chance, strikes happening now but idk how much more people will care

62417947
>>62416450
>it will take years to repair the damages in Russia.
So far, it has taken Russia less than a week to repair crude export terminals. Crude oil export volumes have not been affected.

Refinery damage is harder to see, however it's not nearly as much as the damage in the Gulf, because 50kg warheads simply do not cause much damage

62417949
>>62411825
The price is the price. It reflects a consensus amongst oil traders who are very sophisticated. Why do you think you possess knowledge that they don't? I think some oil stocks can be a good investment but derivatives on the commodities or ETFs that invest in them are a gamble.

62418308
>>62411825
The war hasnt ended and the "ceasefire" is fake and gay

expect $160 a barrel

62418377
Sure, you have the facts on your side. The bears have every world leader on their side, every industrial titan, trillions of dollars of economic output that all depends on oil staying affordable. You're a fool to challenge their ability to continue denying reality, they'll do whatever it takes to keep the numbers healthy.

62420386
>>62418377
That is literally what are those people already doing to keep the price under $100. After a certain point though, they won't be able to hold on anymore.

62421774
>>62413818
>oil barrelies

62421795
>>62416450
>he thinks the real reserves are openly communicated and updated

62423372
>>62421795
Educate me about those super secret subterranean 10 dimensional reserves

62423378
>>62411825
Everytime we have these outsized moves on some random FOTM bullshit e.g GME/BBBY/LUNC/Oil a bunch of you retards develop an emotional attachment to it instead of just trading it and leaving when the attention moves on

62426824
>>62423378
$150 EOY

62428877
>>62411825
How do I invest in oil? I've already filled up large metal tank (40 gallons) with oil.

62428910
I would like to believe the people saying that there are actual supply issues. After all, there's evidence the oil reserves are running out and there are far fewer ships getting out of the strait.

On the other hand I'm a little puzzled at how much the oil price is dropping. Price per barrel is now at pre-war levels, as if nothing has happened (which is definitely not true).

It's like the oil commodity trading market is fake and gay, so when the fakest and gayest faggot of them all (Orange King Migger) speaks, they manipulate the price to whatever he wants. How long can this last, or will being fake and gay work out in the end?

62429038
>>62428910
You need to divorce your 3rd world worshipping 5th columnist world view from your investing strategy. You're more than welcome to worship SneakO and think America is a failed nation running on fumes and that the west will totally fall anyday now but that just isn't a good investing strategy.

62429107
>>62429038
I don't know who sneako is besides the fact that he lives rent free in your head. Thanks for telling me about that.

62431556
>>62411825
How did that thread survife a week?

62431619
>>62411825
You are like the silver baggies saying not selling at $100+

62431639
>>62411825
Sold my short at that 72,7 dip an hour ago and got a long there instead.

62431673
Which oil ETF should I invest into? Tired of tech after finally offloading my MU bags.

62433003
>>62431673
My preference is ETFs based on oil stocks instead of ones that invest in the commodity. I think XOP is a good one if you think WTI will go up as it's based on the producers.

62433007
>>62411825
Price... oh and.. chart

item.title: Have you capitulated on Chainlink yet or are you holding to 0? — 3 replies — 12.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Have you capitulated on Chainlink yet or are you holding to 0?

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-07-01 04:47 UTC

OP

Have you capitulated on Chainlink yet or are you holding to 0? If it's not obvious by now, LINK isn't used or needed for anything, and doesn't give you any sort of stake in the Chainlink company. It's effectively a meme coin with an illusion of "fundamentals".

62433176
>>62433164
Yes I’m the last stink marine on /biz/ that’s held out and I finally capitulated. You can tell big serg he can let it rip now.

62433183
>>62433164
Don't care not selling

62433185
>>62433164
Just sold everything. Good job anon, this was the post that finally did it. Congratulations.

62420685

need to dump then pump to new moon!

62421882

62421933

62422051

62422089
>>62420721

62422734
>>62420669

t. Xrp baggie

62422736
>>62422734
so bullish xrp

62423118
>>62420721

62423202
>>62420669

62423423

62423468
>>62420669

>they increase in price
>they do not rot, last for years

62423665
>>62420691
Id start a MCD position here unironically. PE of 22 meanwhile mcgoyslop is more popular then ever

62423971
>>62423423
The most cringe Boomers hold xrp.
Boomers?
B
Boooo
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

>>62423971

62424378
>>62420669

62424436

62424461

62424480

62424486

62424562

62424593

62424701

62425202

62425633
>>62420669

>>62420880
>>62422734
>>62420669
XRP is outperforming all top tokens since Trump revived it. link is underperforming fucking Terra Luna's death spiraled LUNC. Lol

62425727
>>62425633

62426945
>>62420669
XRP is $1?
Awesome, time to load a truck.

62426966
>>62420669
that $4 burger is clearly a bigger burger compared to the $1.20 burger

62426977
>>62420721
this has ruined my day

62427163
>>62420669
I spent eight years comparing ham sandwiches to xrp and telling people they could trade based on the relative values
No one listened.

Good news.
They both meet at $20 in 2030.
Trade ham sandwiches for xrp now.
Thank me later.

62429395
>>62426977

item.title: Should I get myself a nice, thick and juicy stack of BTC right now, or is this shit crashing further. — 13 replies — 9.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Should I get myself a nice, thick and juicy stack of BTC right now, or is this shit crashing further.

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-07-01 03:30 UTC

OP

Should I get myself a nice, thick and juicy stack of BTC right now, or is this shit crashing further.

Early replies (first 3)

62429628
>>62429620
Go for it, if you're scared it will fall further, just average in over a few weeks or months instead of lump sum

62429630
It will go to single-digit values.

62429700
>>62429620
>Should I get myself a nice, thick and juicy stack of BTC right now, or is this shit crashing further.

No it's crashing further, and what about when Israel does 9/11 again In America this summer, it will drop more, then buy.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62429708
>>62429620
wait til october.

62429711
I love how everything is lining up for a biblical crash in everything.
The oil short squeeze will be the trigger btw, exactly like in 2008.

62429745
>>62429620
patience padawan, there is another

62429772
>>62429620
the fact that there hasn't been a single bounce on these sell-offs says the kikes aren't done yet

62429775
Usd is rising and rates are potentially being raised

What’s the bull case here for crypto?

62429790
>>62429620
I'm not touching this shit while Saylor is sitting on a nuclear bomb.

62429804
>>62429620
The only reason anyone ever bought bitcoin was so they can get rich quick. Everybody is slowly realizing that dream is over, it doesn't go any higher than this because it has no actual value or produce any actual value. Where exactly do you think the price is going? 100k? why would anybody pay 100k for bitcoin anymore. It's over.

62429840
>>62429620
I had the same thought. As such, I am confident that it will plummet another 50%. I recommend waiting.

62433047
>>62429840
is this sam altmans bot? some agi shit

62433049
>>62429620
dca over the next year, trust me we might see some etfs go to insolvent levels

item.title: hey linkies. did you miss me. just got back from another long luxurious vacation paid for by my NON-chainlink crypto gai… — 19 replies — 4.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
hey linkies. did you miss me. just got back from another long luxurious vacation paid for by my NON-chainlink crypto gai…

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-07-01 00:27 UTC

OP

hey linkies. did you miss me. just got back from another long luxurious vacation paid for by my NON-chainlink crypto gains. and what a delicious treat to see when i returned: link struggling to stay above $7 and fat serg canceling the build program. LMAO. you stinkoids just can't catch a break huh. honestly how do ya'll have any hope left at this point. what is going to cause link to break $10, let alone $50? kekkerinos.

Early replies (first 3)

62424357
>>62424306
We've missed you. :(

62424375
After a big pump and dump, assets seldom pump again. It happened once with BTC but it's rare. Link is unlikely to see serious gains again.

62424382
>>62424375
The big banks will pump our bags once they start using LINK in earnest by 2030 to run the entire financial infrastructure.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62424407
>>62424306
Laugh all you want, but the linkies and their friends will be here any minute.

62424429
>>62424407
I'm the only one left

62424443
Glad to hear you had a nice time, kekkarino!

62425371
pain and misery for all stinkies bump

62425397
>>62424306
>>62424357
>>62424375
>>62424382
>>62424407

Fellow link holders we should just ignore this guy.

He is spreading fud about our investment and he is wrong

Can't the mods just ban him?

I mean I know this is 4chan and all and we like to joke and be mean to one another but as a multiple year long chainlink holder I have my limits

Anyway i'm back to my job cleaning toilets now

62425406
>>62424382
i find great satisfction that there are people like you who never took any profit at all...

i get great pleasure knowing it just gets worse and worse for you as time goes on,


i get great pleasure knowing your lasts breaths will be that you sadly had to pass away before link pumped

62425455
>>62424306
welcome back, all the marines have gone extinct or are the verge of, the scam has unraveled

62425517
>>62424306

62428898
burp

62428960
>>62424306
>>62428898
We're not buying your bags, linkie. Stop creating threads about your shitcoin.

62429180
>>62428960
don't you ever call me a stinkie ever again, you hag

62429262
>>62425406

62429780
